error.signature: Fine-tuned model loses base capabilities after training on domain-specific data

verdict.summary: Fine-tuning on narrow domain data causes catastrophic forgetting of base model capabilities. The model becomes an expert in your domain but loses general reasoning, instruction following, or other skills.

dead_ends.action: Fine-tune on domain data only without including general examples
dead_ends.why_fails: Training exclusively on domain data overwrites general capabilities. The model forgets how to follow instructions, do math, or reason about non-domain topics.

dead_ends.action: Fine-tune for many epochs to maximize domain performance
dead_ends.why_fails: More epochs = more forgetting. 3-5 epochs is usually optimal. Beyond that, validation loss increases while training loss decreases (overfitting).

workarounds.action: Mix domain data with general instruction-following examples (80/20 ratio)
Use LoRA/QLoRA instead of full fine-tuning to preserve base capabilities
workarounds.how: Fine-tune only adapter weights (r=16, alpha=32). Base model weights remain frozen. Merge on inference.

workarounds.action: Use RAG + few-shot prompting before resorting to fine-tuning
workarounds.how: Often retrieval-augmented generation with examples achieves similar quality without forgetting risks
